conversations.title: Smart Learning
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 1b and 1c · Conversation 1

lines.speaker: lisa
lines.text: Teng Fei, you always do well in maths. How do you study for it?

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: For me, I think the best way is to do maths problems. I found a learning app for that. Let me show you.

lines.speaker: lisa
lines.text: Cool! How does it work?

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: You begin by taking a test. It will find your weak points. Then it gives exercises to work on them.

lines.speaker: lisa
lines.text: Oh, good. That's smart.

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: Exactly!

conversations.instruction: Listen to the conversation. How does Teng Fei study for maths?

questions.prompt: What does Teng Fei use to study maths?
What does the app do after the test?
options: It gives exercises for weak points

conversations.title: Smart Learning
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 1b and 1c · Conversation 2

lines.speaker: adam
lines.text: Hi, Fu Xing. I wonder if you could help me with my history study. You always get good marks in history. How do you do it?

lines.speaker: fu_xing
lines.text: Sure, Adam. I study by taking notes a lot.

lines.speaker: adam
lines.text: What do you mean by that?

lines.speaker: fu_xing
lines.text: Well, for historical events, I usually make a timeline and test myself on the key points. I like using mind maps. I think they can help me better understand historical events.

lines.speaker: adam
lines.text: Sounds interesting. Thanks!

conversations.instruction: Listen to Adam and Fu Xing. How does Fu Xing study history?

questions.prompt: How does Fu Xing mainly study history?
What tools does Fu Xing mention?

conversations.title: Smart Learning
conversations.subtitle: Conversation 3

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: What is your secret to learning physics well, Peter?

lines.speaker: peter
lines.text: Well, Ella, I like doing hands-on experiments.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: How does that help?

lines.speaker: peter
lines.text: You know, everything in physics connects with something in real life. By doing hands-on experiments, I can see that connection.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Wow, I see! Many famous physicists also started doing experiments when they were young.

lines.speaker: peter
lines.text: Exactly!

conversations.instruction: Listen to Ella and Peter. What is Peter’s secret to learning physics?

questions.prompt: How does Peter like to learn physics?

options: To see how physics connects with real life

conversations.title: Smart Learning
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 2b and 2c

lines.speaker: mr_smith
lines.text: Welcome to the English club! Today's question is: What are some good ways to learn English?

lines.speaker: fu_xing
lines.text: I like to learn new words by using flashcards.

lines.speaker: mr_smith
lines.text: Yes, flashcards are easy to carry around and let you review the words.

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: It's also good to note down useful expressions and word groups.

lines.speaker: mr_smith
lines.text: Right. It's helpful to put words into meaningful groups. I also want to know whether you read English books outside class.

lines.speaker: yaming
lines.text: Yes. I like reading a lot, both in English and Chinese.

lines.speaker: mr_smith
lines.text: Good. I believe reading is important for learning in general.

lines.speaker: meimei
lines.text: I like reading too. But I can't read very fast.

lines.speaker: fu_xing
lines.text: I guess maybe you need to read books that suit your English level.

lines.speaker: mr_smith
lines.text: Yes, choose something interesting and easy to read. Read something every day and read widely. The more you read, the faster and better you'll become. Any other ideas?

conversations.instruction: Listen to the English club discussion. What good ways to learn English are mentioned?

questions.prompt: What does Fu Xing use to learn new words?
What does Teng Fei suggest noting down?
What reading advice does Mr Smith give Meimei?
